name: Thin Kit
explanation: A cheap, thin, dry 12-bit sampling drum box in the Casio RZ-1 lineage. Where the factory's other boxes are warm, crunchy or glassy, this one is small and papery: a short thin kick, a papery snare, tight hats, a clicky rimshot, a small cowbell and a dry clap. A mild 12-bit crush plus a global BRIGHT high-pass give the cheap-PCM character. Like the original it is a preset PATTERN box: hold a note to start an internal 16-step pop groove, release to stop and let the tails ring. Controls: Tune (kick pitch), Decay, Snap (snare noise vs body), Bright (global high-pass / thinness), Accent and Level.
